content_id:
Pembahasan Sharh al-Aqa'id an-Nasafiyyah tentang iman (iman), kenabian (nubuwwah), dan konsensus akidah komunitas mencakup aspek-aspek teologi Islam yang paling langsung relevan dengan kehidupan beragama sehari-hari. Bagian-bagian ini termasuk yang paling penting secara praktis bagi umat Islam dan telah menghasilkan diskusi yang berkelanjutan selama berabad-abad penggunaan karya tersebut.
Mengenai iman, posisi Maturidi sebagaimana disajikan oleh an-Nasafi dan dijelaskan oleh at-Taftazani berpendapat bahwa iman terutama terdiri dari keyakinan batin dan penegasan lisan (tasdiq bil-qalb wa iqrar bil-lisan). Ini berbeda dari beberapa presentasi dalam tradisi Asy'ari yang menekankan penegasan lisan, dan dari posisi Athari yang memasukkan tindakan sebagai komponen iman. Mazhab Maturidi berpendapat bahwa seorang Muslim yang memiliki keyakinan batin yang tulus tetapi gagal melakukan tindakan-tindakan tetap seorang Muslim - meskipun orang yang berdosa - karena tindakan-tindakan tersebut, betapa pun wajibnya, bukanlah komponen iman itu sendiri.
At-Taftazani menjelaskan bahwa iman tidak bertambah atau berkurang dalam pandangan Maturidi - iman adalah keyakinan yang lengkap, dan keyakinan yang lengkap tidak mengakui derajat. Ini adalah poin perbedaan lain dari tradisi Athari, yang berpendapat bahwa iman bertambah dengan ketaatan dan berkurang dengan dosa. Posisi Maturidi adalah bahwa yang bertambah dan berkurang adalah kualitas dan buah-buah iman - ketenangan, dedikasi, manfaat spiritual - bukan iman itu sendiri, yang hadir atau tidak hadir sebagai keyakinan yang lengkap.
Mengenai kenabian, an-Nasafi menegaskan garis kenabian yang lengkap dari Adam hingga Muhammad, kewajiban untuk percaya kepada semua nabi dan menghormati mereka, serta finalitas misi Muhammad. Komentar at-Taftazani menjelaskan sifat-sifat yang diperlukan para nabi - kejujuran, kepercayaan, dan kecerdasan mereka - dan mendiskusikan bagaimana perlindungan kenabian dari dosa (isma) harus dipahami. Posisi Maturidi melindungi para nabi dari penipuan yang disengaja dan dosa-dosa besar tetapi mengizinkan kesalahan-kesalahan kecil dan kesalahan dalam penilaian duniawi.
Bagian-bagian tentang komunitas membahas status para sahabat Nabi, legitimasi empat khalifah pertama, dan sikap yang tepat terhadap perselisihan sektarian dalam komunitas Muslim. Pernyataan-pernyataan an-Nasafi dalam hal-hal ini adalah bagian dari konsensus Sunni klasik tentang penghormatan yang diwajibkan kepada para sahabat dan keabsahan kekhalifahan awal, dan komentar at-Taftazani menempatkannya dalam perdebatan yang lebih besar antara interpretasi Sunni dan Syiah tentang sejarah Islam awal. Posisi karya ini jelas dan konsisten dengan tradisi Sunni standar sambil menghindari ketajaman polemik yang tidak perlu.
content_en:
Sharh al-Aqa'id an-Nasafiyyah's treatment of faith (iman), prophethood (nubuwwah), and the community's creedal consensus covers the aspects of Islamic theology most directly relevant to everyday religious life. These sections are among the most practically important for Muslim believers and have generated sustained discussion across the centuries of the work's use.

On faith, the Maturidi position as presented by an-Nasafi and explained by at-Taftazani holds that faith consists primarily of inner conviction and verbal affirmation (tasdiq bil-qalb wa iqrar bil-lisan). This differs from some presentations in the Ash'ari tradition that emphasize verbal affirmation, and from the Athari position that includes action as a component of faith. The Maturidi school held that a Muslim who has sincere inner conviction but fails to perform actions remains a Muslim — though a sinful one — because the actions, however obligatory, are not constituents of faith itself.

At-Taftazani explains that faith does not increase or decrease in the Maturidi view — faith is complete conviction, and complete conviction does not admit of degrees. This is another point of difference from the Athari tradition, which held that faith increases with obedience and decreases with sin. The Maturidi position is that what increases and decreases is the quality and fruits of faith — the tranquility, the dedication, the spiritual benefit — not faith itself, which is either present or absent as complete conviction.

On prophethood, an-Nasafi affirms the complete prophetic line from Adam to Muhammad, the obligation to believe in all prophets and to honor them, and the finality of Muhammad's mission. At-Taftazani's commentary explains the necessary attributes of the prophets — their truthfulness, trustworthiness, and intelligence — and discusses how prophetic preservation from sin (isma) should be understood. The Maturidi position preserved prophets from deliberate deception and major sins but allowed for minor errors and mistakes in worldly judgment.

The sections on the community address the status of the companions of the Prophet, the legitimacy of the first four caliphs, and the proper attitude toward sectarian disputes within the Muslim community. An-Nasafi's statements on these matters are part of the classical Sunni consensus on the respect due to the companions and the validity of the early caliphate, and at-Taftazani's commentary situates them within the larger debate between Sunni and Shia interpretations of early Islamic history. The work's position is clear and consistent with the standard Sunni tradition while avoiding gratuitous polemical sharpness.
content_ar: Sharh al-Aqa'id an-Nasafiyyah's treatment of الإيمان (الإيمان), prophethood (nubuwwah), and the الجماعة's creedal consensus covers the aspects of Islamic theology most directly relevant to everyday religious الحياة. These sections are among the most practically المهم for مسلم المؤمنون and have generated sustained discussion across the centuries of the work's use. On الإيمان, the الماتريدي position as presented by an-Nasafi and explained by at-Taftazani holds that الإيمان consists primarily of inner conviction and verbal affirmation (tasdiq bil-qalb wa iqrar bil-lisan). This differs from some presentations in the الأشعري التراث that emphasize verbal affirmation, and from the الأثري position that includes action as a component of الإيمان. The الماتريدي school held that a مسلم who has sincere inner conviction but fails to perform actions remains a مسلم — though a sinful one — لأن the actions, غير أن obligatory, are not constituents of الإيمان itself. At-Taftazani explains that الإيمان does not increase or decrease in the الماتريدي view — الإيمان is complete conviction, and complete conviction does not admit of degrees. This is another point of difference from the الأثري التراث, which held that الإيمان increases with obedience and decreases with الذنب. The الماتريدي position is that what increases and decreases is the quality and fruits of الإيمان — the السكينة, the dedication, the spiritual benefit — not الإيمان itself, which is either present or absent as complete conviction. On prophethood, an-Nasafi affirms the complete prophetic line from آدم to محمد ﷺ, the الالتزام to believe in all الأنبياء and to الشرف them, and the finality of محمد ﷺ's mission. At-Taftazani's commentary explains the necessary attributes of the الأنبياء — their truthfulness, trustworthiness, and intelligence — and discusses how prophetic preservation from الذنب (isma) should be understood. The الماتريدي position preserved الأنبياء from deliberate deception and major الذنوب but allowed for minor errors and mistakes in worldly judgment. The sections on the الجماعة address the status of the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ, the legitimacy of the أولاً four caliphs, and the proper attitude toward sectarian disputes within the مسلم الجماعة. An-Nasafi's statements on these matters are part of the الكلاسيكي Sunni consensus on the الاحترام بسبب the الصحابة and the validity of the early الخلافة, and at-Taftazani's commentary situates them within the larger debate between Sunni and Shia interpretations of early Islamic history. The work's position is clear and consistent with the standard Sunni التراث بينما avoiding gratuitous polemical sharpness.
